commit.message:
feat: persistent progress for saved setlists (#53)

* feat: persistent progress for saved setlists

Add active setlist tracking so checked-card progress persists
reliably across page reloads and config changes.

Key changes:
- Track activeSetlistId (persisted in localStorage) — loading a
  saved setlist activates it, giving it a stable storage key
  (setlist:${id}) instead of the volatile config-derived key
- Migrate legacy progress from old dynamic keys on first load
- Lift savedSetlists state into useCollectionState for centralized
  save/delete/update management
- Add Save/Save As/New buttons with active setlist visual indicator
- Add uniqueArtOnly to SavedSetlist type (was missing)
- Clean up progress key when deleting a setlist
- Deactivate active setlist when loading a share URL

Progress auto-saves because useCheckedCards already writes on
every toggle — the stable key prevents data loss from key drift.

commit.message:
Unify reports into all/actionable.html with URL-param filters (#78)

* Add community, quick-wins, and consider-closing filters to unified view

Add 3 new report-mode filter checkboxes to all/actionable.html:
- Community awaiting review: is_community AND next_action matches review
- Quick wins: Ready to merge: next_action matches Ready to merge
- Consider closing: (age>90d AND stale>30d) OR (age>180d AND stale>14d)

These filters are URL-authoritative only (not localStorage-persisted) to
prevent stale state from breaking links. They compose with all existing
filters (user, area, repo, maintainer, easy-action).

Stale mode defaults to sorting by days_since_update desc. Summary bar
shows active report-mode filter names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Write redirect stubs for non-actionable reports, update index links

Build-Reports.ps1: community, quick-wins, and stale-close reports now
write lightweight redirect stubs pointing to all/actionable.html with
appropriate URL params (e.g. ?repo=runtime&community=true). The filter
predicates still run to compute counts for meta.json. Per-repo
actionable.html remains server-rendered for fast first paint.

Build-Index.ps1: community/quick-wins/consider-closing links now point
to the unified view (all/actionable.html?repo={slug}&{filter}=true).
The Most Actionable row still links to per-repo actionable.html.
Layout, counts, sparklines, and all other content are unchanged.

Nav links in per-repo actionable.html are updated to point to the
unified view for redirected report types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Pester parity tests for report filter predicates

Tests verify that community, quick-wins, and stale-close filter
predicates produce correct results with synthetic test data. Also
validates:
- Redirect stub code exists in Build-Reports.ps1
- Index link mappings in Build-Index.ps1
- JS predicates in all/actionable.html match PowerShell predicates
- Report-mode filters are URL-only (no localStorage)
- Edge cases: exact boundary values excluded (> not >=)
- Stale sort order (days_since_update descending)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix quick-wins case sensitivity, savedUser restore, improve tests

Bug fixes from rubber-duck review:
- Quick-wins JS filter now uses /Ready to merge/i (case-insensitive)
  to match PowerShell -match semantics
- Report-mode URLs (?stale=true etc) no longer restore savedUser from
  localStorage when no user= param is present, preventing shared links
  from silently becoming user-scoped views

Test improvements (22 tests, up from 16):
- Added overlapping PR categories (community+quickwin, stale+community)
- Added null field edge cases (is_community=null, next_action=null)
- Added case-insensitive quick-wins match test
- Added filter composition tests (AND semantics)
- Fixed localStorage non-persistence test to be more specific

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reorganize filter layout: checkboxes on one row, lozenges inline

Move all checkboxes (user-context, maintainer-context, and report-mode)
into a single row below the username input. Move recent user tiles
(lozenges) to the right of the username input on the same row, instead
of a separate row below. This gives a more compact, consistent layout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore recent-user lozenges to original standalone row

Move lozenges back to their own row below the username input (above
checkboxes) with original smaller/lighter styling, instead of inline
with the input controls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Match input/button font-size to checkbox labels (0.85em) with reduced padding
- Remove redundant 'Showing PRs for @X' summary bar (user visible in input above)
- Summary bar now shows only PR count/repo breakdown
- Involves checkbox always visible: checked+disabled when no user, enabled when user set

- Redirect stubs now preserve existing query params and hash from the

- Add 7 new Pester tests for old bookmark redirects: docs/all/ stub,

- Update T5 in Test-CiValidation.ps1 to validate redirect stub

- K1: involves-label is now always visible (checked+disabled when no user)
- K3-K5: summary bar shows PR count/breakdown, not filter descriptions
- K6: clear maintainer filter via checkbox uncheck, not clear link

- buildCurrentUrl() now includes location.hash so replaceState doesn't strip fragments
- Stale localStorage test regex checks for 'stale' not just 'staleMode'

commit.message:
fix: have all bots subscribe to all relays and follow all users (#75)

## Problem

`subscribeToRelays` only used the **first bot** to subscribe to relays.
Because the `relays` table previously had a unique constraint on `url`
alone, all subsequent bots were skipped once a relay URL was already
recorded — meaning only one of the 40+ bots would ever join a relay.
`followAccounts` was already correct (each bot follows each user
individually), so only the relay subscription logic needed fixing.

## Changes

### `src/lib/schema.ts`
- Added `botUsername` column to the `relays` table.
- Changed the unique constraint from `url` alone to `(botUsername,
url)`, enabling per-bot relay tracking.

### `src/lib/db.ts`
- Updated `RelayRow` type to include `botUsername`.
- `upsertRelay` now accepts a `botUsername` parameter.
- `getAllRelays` now accepts an optional `botUsername` filter.
- `getAcceptedRelays` deduplicates results by `inboxUrl` to avoid
delivering to the same relay inbox multiple times now that each relay
has one row per bot.
- `removeRelay` is now scoped per-bot.
- Added `getRelayStatusSummary` and `getFollowingStatusSummary` helpers
used by the new status page.

### `src/lib/federation.ts`
- `subscribeToRelays` now resolves each relay actor once (using the
first bot as a signing identity) and sends a Follow from **every** bot.
- The already-subscribed check uses `"botUsername:relayUrl"` composite
keys so new bots always get properly subscribed even if the relay URL
already exists in the table.

### `src/app/status/page.tsx` (new)
- Adds a `/status` page showing three sections:
- **Relay Subscriptions** — per-relay accepted/pending/rejected counts
with a `bots / total` ratio (highlighted amber when not all bots have
subscribed).
  - **Account Follows** — same breakdown for each configured handle.
- **RSS Feeds** — table of all bots with their display name and feed
URL.

### `src/app/layout.tsx`
- Adds a **Status** link to the nav bar next to the existing **Stats**
link.

### `drizzle/0008_relay_per_bot.sql` (new migration)
- Truncates the `relays` table (data will be re-established on next
startup).
- Drops the old `relays_url_unique` constraint.
- Adds the `bot_username` column (`NOT NULL`).
- Adds the new `relays_bot_username_url_unique` constraint on
`(bot_username, url)`.

### `drizzle/meta/` (generated)
- `0008_snapshot.json` and updated `_journal.json` generated by
`drizzle-kit generate --name=relay_per_bot`.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: icco <20201+icco@users.noreply.github.com>

when experiment status was not 'running'. However:

where experiment directories are deleted.

commit.message:
fix: use article_path.txt for precise blog deploy instead of *.md glob (#274)

Previously the 'Publish to Blog Repository' step used:
  cp ../output/*.md _posts/
This could accidentally deploy old articles, quarantined articles,
or non-post .md files (like audit reports) that accumulate in output/.

The Generate Article step already writes the validated article path to
article_path.txt. Use that file to copy only the specific validated
article, its chart, and its image.

If article_path.txt is missing or the file doesn't exist, the deploy
fails immediately rather than deploying the wrong article.

Fixes #273

Rename input keydown events were bubbling to the workspace tree handler, where Backspace/Delete is bound to file deletion. This caused delete prompts while editing filenames.\n\nStop propagation on rename input keydown and skip tree-level keyboard handling for editable targets or active rename mode, preserving existing shortcuts outside rename flows.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove auth requirement from system-health endpoint and improve frontend resilience (#196)

The system-health endpoint was gated behind authenticateRequest() but the
shell runtime health hook polls it without an Authorization header, causing
every health check to fail with 401 in production.

- Remove auth gate from system-health.js (only returns non-sensitive status)
- Skip retries for 401/403 in use-runtime-health.ts (permanent failures)

commit.message:
feat: single-cycle implement → review → fix → merge (#78)

Remove the multi-round review/fix loop. The lifecycle now runs exactly
one cycle:

**implement → review → fix → merge**

No re-review after the fix. The review catches catastrophic bugs, the
fix addresses them, then we merge. Iterating further wastes tokens and
time without converging (as seen in PR #76's 8-round cycle where the bot
contradicted itself).

### Changes
- Remove `MAX_FIX_ITERATIONS` and `max_iterations` status
- Simplify `implement_issue` to linear flow (was 200+ line loop → clean
sequence)
- Review errors don't block merge (advisory)
- Update bot-review skill: "There is only one review round"
- Update bot-implement skill: document one-cycle contract
- 120 tests pass, net -114 lines

---------

commit.message:
Fix led.osc: use read_nonblock poll loop for Linux compatibility

l_term_raw() sets VMIN=0/VTIME=0 on Linux freestanding, making
read() non-blocking. read_byte(0) returns -1 immediately with no
input, causing an infinite busy loop that never processes keys.

Fix: use read_nonblock() + sleep_ms(20) poll loop for the main
input, and read_key_timeout() (50ms timeout) for escape sequence
bytes. Works on both Windows (blocking console) and Linux
(non-blocking after term_raw).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
